name: Sopaipillas 2
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve the yeast in warm water in a large mixing bowl.
2. In another bowl, combine milk, lard, salt, and sugar. Heat to 110°F and add to the dissolved yeast.
3. Beat in 3 cups of all-purpose flour and all of the whole wheat flour. Add about 1/2 cup of all-purpose flour and mix until a stiff, sticky dough forms.
4. Place the dough on a floured board and knead, adding more flour as needed, until the dough is smooth and nonsticky.
5. Place the dough in a greased bowl, turning to grease the top, and cover. Let stand at room temperature for 1 hour.
6. Punch down the dough. Cover and chill as long as overnight.
7. Knead the dough on a lightly floured board to expel air.
8. Roll the dough out, a portion at a time, to slightly less than 1/8 inch thick.
9. Cut into 2" x 5-inch rectangles or 3-inch squares for appetizers.
10. Place on lightly floured pans and lightly cover. If you work quickly, you can let the cut sopaipillas stay at room temperature up to 5 minutes; otherwise, refrigerate them until all are ready to fry.
11. Heat 1-2 inches of oil in a deep frying pan or kettle to 350°F on a deep fat thermometer.
12. Fry 2-3 at a time. When the bread begins to puff, gently push it into the hot oil several times to help it puff more evenly.
13. Turn several times and cook just until pale gold on both sides, about 1-2 minutes total.
14. Drain on paper towels. Serve immediately or place in a warm oven until all are fried. If made ahead, cool, cover, and chill or freeze. To reheat, bake uncovered in a 300°F oven, turning once, just until warm, 5-8 minutes. Does not exceed heat. Makes 2 dozen large sopaipillas or about 4 dozen small ones.
